title: pickeringia montana var. tomentosa
intro: Pickeringia montana var. tomentosa, also known as woolly Indian clover or hairy prairie clover, is a plant species that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Pickeringia montana var. tomentosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
